subject: Extraction and 3D Auto-Refine Questions
body:
Hello,

I'm back again with a few questions after working on one of my own personal 
datasets.

Alignment: For my own data, I've had issues using the Aretomo and IMOD 
wrappers in WarpTools.  I had no issues using the Wrappers with the 
apoferritin test dataset but when I use the wrappers with my data, it 
usually gives me very poor alignments (very poor or off looking 
tomograms).  When I've exported the data out to use the automated Etomo 
alignments and reimported them back into Warp, the alignments look great 
and so do the tomograms.   There's no error when I use the Wrappers so it 
makes me think something is either off with the settings I run the wrappers 
with or maybe even my data.  Or are there any common issues with the using 
the wrappers?

Extraction: 
(1) I see that for WarpTools particle extraction you can extract the 
particles as either 2D or 3D images, when would you want to use one over 
the other? 

(2) I used crYOLO for particle picking my own dataset and found that most 
of the average.mrcs files looked good, but a few averages were noticeably 
off center (~10-20 pixels).  I increased my box size a bit to compensate 
for this but was wondering if this is common for any non-WarpTool pickers? 
Or did this happen since I did not spend time refining the model?

3D Auto-Refine:
(1) Are there any differences (or good rules of thumb) for running 3D 
auto-refine on subtomogram averages that I should be aware of? For my 
recent data set, I ran 3D Autorefine and it looked decent up to the second 
to last iteration (~22 A with rough but expected features) but the final 
iteration looked really bad (8 A reported but it was all noise and no 
observable features).   I'm not sure if it's an issue of my particles, 
parameters or lack of particles (only 155 particles of an icosahedral 
virus).  

Any advice is appreciated!

From,
Kyle
P.S.  Thanks for answering my question last time Alister!
